classes.name: AcousticEmitter
classes.comment: information about a specific UA emitter. Section 6.2.2.

fields.name: acousticSystemName
fields.comment: the system for a particular UA emitter, and an enumeration

fields.name: acousticFunction
fields.comment: The function of the acoustic system

fields.name: acousticIDNumber
fields.comment: The UA emitter identification number relative to a specific system

classes.name: AggregateIdentifier
classes.comment: The unique designation of each aggrgate in an exercise is specified by an aggregate identifier record. The aggregate ID is not an entity and shall not be treated as such. Section 6.2.3.

fields.name: simulationAddress
fields.comment: Simulation address, ie site and application, the first two fields of the entity ID

fields.name: aggregateID
fields.comment: the aggregate ID, an object identifier

classes.name: AggregateMarking
classes.comment: Specifies the character set used in the first byte, followed by up to 31 characters of text data. Section 6.2.4.

classes.name: AggregateType
classes.comment: Identifies the type and organization of an aggregate. Section 6.2.5

fields.name: aggregateKind
fields.comment: Grouping criterion used to group the aggregate. Enumeration from EBV document

fields.name: domain
fields.comment: Domain of entity (air, surface, subsurface, space, etc) Zero means domain does not apply.

fields.name: country
fields.comment: country to which the design of the entity is attributed

fields.name: specificInfo
fields.comment: specific info based on subcategory field. specific is a reserved word in sql.

fields.name: extra
fields.comment: extra information needed to describe the aggregate

classes.name: AngularVelocityVector
classes.comment: Angular velocity measured in radians per second out each of the entity's own coordinate axes. Order of measurement is angular velocity around the x, y, and z axis of the entity. The positive direction is determined by the right hand rule. Section 6.2.7

classes.name: AntennaLocation
classes.comment: Location of the radiating portion of the antenna, specified in world coordinates and entity coordinates. Section 6.2.8

fields.name: antennaLocation
fields.comment: Location of the radiating portion of the antenna in world    coordinates

fields.name: relativeAntennaLocation
fields.comment: Location of the radiating portion of the antenna     in entity coordinates

classes.name: BeamAntennaPattern
classes.comment: Used when the antenna pattern type field has a value of 1. Specifies the direction, pattern, and polarization of radiation from an antenna. Section 6.2.9.2

fields.name: beamDirection
fields.comment: The rotation that transforms the reference coordinate sytem into the beam coordinate system. Either world coordinates or entity coordinates may be used as the reference coordinate system, as specified by the reference system field of the antenna pattern record.

fields.name: azimuthBeamwidth
fields.comment: Full width of the beam to the -3dB power density points in the x-y plane of the beam coordinnate system.  Elevation beamwidth is represented by a 32 bit floating point number in units of radians.

fields.name: elevationBeamwidth
fields.comment: This field shall specify the full width of the beam to the –3 dB power density points in the x-z plane of the beam coordinate system. Elevation beamwidth shall be represented by a 32-bit floating point number in units of radians.

fields.name: referenceSystem
fields.comment: The reference coordinate system wrt which beam direction  is specified. This field should not change over the duration of an exercise. World coordindate systemis prefered for exercises. The entity coordinate system should be used only when highly directional antennas must be precisely modeled.

fields.name: ez
fields.comment: This field shall specify the magnitude of the Z-component (in beam coordinates) of the Electrical field at some arbitrary single point in the main beam and in the far field of the antenna.

fields.name: ex
fields.comment: This field shall specify the magnitude of the X-component (in beam coordinates) of the Electri- cal field at some arbitrary single point in the main beam and in the far field of the antenna.

fields.name: phase
fields.comment: This field shall specify the phase angle between EZ and EX in radians. If fully omni-direc- tional antenna is modeled using beam pattern type one, the omni-directional antenna shall be repre- sented by beam direction Euler angles psi, theta, and phi of zero, an azimuth beamwidth of 2PI, and an elevation beamwidth of PI

classes.name: Association
classes.comment: An entity's associations with other entities and/or locations. For each association, this record shall specify the type of the association, the associated entity's EntityID and/or the associated location's world coordinates. This record may be used (optionally) in a transfer transaction to send internal state data from the divesting simulation to the acquiring simulation (see 5.9.4). This record may also be used for other purposes. Section 6.2.9

fields.name: associationType
fields.comment: This field shall indicate the type of association. It shall be represented by an 8-bit enumeration. Values for this field are found in Section 14 of SISO-REF-010

fields.name: associatedEntityID
fields.comment: identity of associated entity. If none, NO_SPECIFIC_ENTITY

classes.name: Attribute
classes.comment: Used to convey information for one or more attributes. Attributes conform to the standard variable record format of 6.2.82. Section 6.2.10. NOT COMPLETE

fields.name: recordType
fields.comment: The record type for this attribute. Enumeration

fields.name: recordLength
fields.comment: Total length of the record in octets. The record shall end on a 64-bit boundary after any padding. = 6 + K + P, where K is the total length of records and P is padding, and 6 is the integer + short

fields.name: recordSpecificFields
fields.comment: The attribute data format conforming to that specified by the record type. K bytes long

classes.name: BeamData
classes.comment: Describes the scan volue of an emitter beam. Section 6.2.11.

fields.name: beamAzimuthCenter
fields.comment: Specifies the beam azimuth an elevation centers and corresponding half-angles to describe the scan volume

fields.name: beamAzimuthSweep
fields.comment: Specifies the beam azimuth sweep to determine scan volume

fields.name: beamElevationCenter
fields.comment: Specifies the beam elevation center to determine scan volume

fields.name: beamElevationSweep
fields.comment: Specifies the beam elevation sweep to determine scan volume

fields.name: beamSweepSync
fields.comment: allows receiver to synchronize its regenerated scan pattern to that of the emmitter. Specifies the percentage of time a scan is through its pattern from its origion.

classes.name: ChangeOptions
classes.comment: This is wrong and breaks serialization. See section 6.2.13 aka B.2.41

classes.name: BeamStatus
classes.comment: Information related to the status of a beam. This is contained in the beam status field of the electromagnitec emission PDU. The first bit determines whether the beam is active (0) or deactivated (1). Section 6.2.12.

fields.name: beamState
fields.comment: First bit zero means beam is active, first bit = 1 means deactivated. The rest is padding.

classes.name: ClockTime
classes.comment: Time measurements that exceed one hour are represented by this record. The first field is the hours since the unix epoch (Jan 1 1970, used by most Unix systems and java) and the second field the timestamp units since the top of the hour. Section 6.2.14

fields.name: timePastHour
fields.comment: Time past the hour, in timestamp form

classes.name: DirectedEnergyDamage
classes.comment: Damage sustained by an entity due to directed energy. Location of the damage based on a relative x,y,z location from the center of the entity. Section 6.2.15.2

fields.name: damageLocation
fields.comment: location of damage, relative to center of entity

fields.name: damageDiameter
fields.comment: Size of damaged area, in meters.

fields.name: temperature
fields.comment: average temp of the damaged area, in degrees celsius. If firing entitty does not model this, use a value of -273.15

fields.name: fireEventID
fields.comment: For any component damage resulting this field shall be set to the fire event ID from that PDU.

classes.name: DataFilterRecord
classes.comment: identify which of the optional data fields are contained in the Minefield Data PDU or requested in the Minefield Query PDU. This is a 32-bit record. For each field, true denotes that the data is requested or present and false denotes that the data is neither requested nor present. Section 6.2.16

classes.name: UnsignedDISInteger
classes.comment: container class not in specification

classes.name: DataQueryDatumSpecification
classes.comment: List of fixed and variable datum ID records. Section 6.2.17

fields.name: fixedDatumIDList
fields.comment: variable length list fixed datum IDs

fields.name: variableDatumIDList
fields.comment: variable length list variable datum IDs

classes.name: DatumSpecification
classes.comment: List of fixed and variable datum records. Section 6.2.18

fields.name: variableDatumIDList
fields.comment: variable length list variable datums. See 6.2.93

classes.name: MunitionDescriptor
classes.comment: Represents the firing or detonation of a munition. Section 6.2.19.2

fields.name: munitionType
fields.comment: What munition was used in the burst

fields.name: quantity
fields.comment: how many of the munition were fired

fields.name: rate
fields.comment: rate at which the munition was fired

classes.name: ExplosionDescriptor
classes.comment: Explosion of a non-munition. Section 6.2.19.3

fields.name: explodingObject
fields.comment: Type of the object that exploded. See 6.2.30

fields.name: explosiveMaterial
fields.comment: Material that exploded. Can be grain dust, tnt, gasoline, etc. Enumeration

fields.name: explosiveForce
fields.comment: Force of explosion, in equivalent KG of TNT

classes.name: ExpendableDescriptor
classes.comment: Burst of chaff or expendible device. Section 6.2.19.4

fields.name: expendableType
fields.comment: Type of the object that exploded

classes.name: DirectedEnergyAreaAimpoint
classes.comment: DE Precision Aimpoint Record. NOT COMPLETE. Section 6.2.20.2

fields.name: beamAntennaPatternRecordCount
fields.comment: Number of beam antenna pattern records

fields.name: directedEnergyTargetEnergyDepositionRecordCount
fields.comment: Number of DE target energy depositon records

fields.name: beamAntennaParameterList
fields.comment: list of beam antenna records. See 6.2.9.2

fields.name: directedEnergyTargetEnergyDepositionRecordList
fields.comment: list of DE target deposition records. See 6.2.21.4

classes.name: DirectedEnergyPrecisionAimpoint
classes.comment: DE Precision Aimpoint Record. Section 6.2.20.3

fields.name: targetSpotLocation
fields.comment: Position of Target Spot in World Coordinates.

fields.name: targetSpotEntityLocation
fields.comment: Position (meters) of Target Spot relative to Entity Position.

fields.name: targetEntityID
fields.comment: Unique ID of the target entity.

fields.name: targetComponentID
fields.comment: Target Component ID ENUM, same as in DamageDescriptionRecord.

fields.name: beamSpotCrossSectionSemiMajorAxis
fields.comment: Beam Spot Cross Section Semi-Major Axis.

fields.name: beamSpotCrossSectionSemiMinorAxis
fields.comment: Beam Spot Cross Section Semi-Major Axis.

fields.name: beamSpotCrossSectionOrientationAngle
fields.comment: Beam Spot Cross Section Orientation Angle.

classes.name: DirectedEnergyTargetEnergyDeposition
classes.comment: DE energy depostion properties for a target entity. Section 6.2.20.4

fields.name: targetEntityID
fields.comment: Unique ID of the target entity.

classes.name: BlankingSector
classes.comment: The Blanking Sector attribute record may be used to convey persistent areas within a scan volume where emitter power for a specific active emitter beam is reduced to an insignificant value. Section 6.2.21.2

fields.name: recordLength
fields.comment: the length of the Blanking Sector attribute record in octets.

fields.name: emitterNumber
fields.comment: indicates the emitter system for which the blanking sector values are applicable

fields.name: beamNumber
fields.comment: indicates the beam for which the blanking sector values are applicable.

fields.name: stateIndicator
fields.comment: indicate if blanking sector data have changed since issuance of the last Blanking Sector attribute record for this beam, if the Blanking Sector attribute record beam has ceased

fields.name: leftAzimuth
fields.comment: This field is provided to indicate the left-most azimuth (clockwise in radians) for which emitted power is reduced. This angle is measured in the X-Y plane of the radar's entity coor- dinate system (see 1.4.3). The range of permissible values is 0 to 2PI, with zero pointing in the X- direction.

fields.name: rightAzimuth
fields.comment: Indicate the right-most azimuth (clockwise in radians) for which emitted power is reduced. This angle is measured in the X-Y plane of the radar's entity coordinate system (see 1.4.3). The range of permissible values is 0 to 2PI , with zero pointing in the X- direction.

fields.name: lowerElevation
fields.comment: This field is provided to indicate the lowest elevation (in radians) for which emit- ted power is reduced. This angle is measured positive upward with respect to the X-Y plane of the radar's entity coordinate system (see 1.4.3). The range of permissible values is -PI/2 to PI/2

fields.name: upperElevation
fields.comment: This field is provided to indicate the highest elevation (in radians) for which emitted power is reduced. This angle is measured positive upward with respect to the X-Y plane of the radar's entitycoordinatesystem(see1.4.3). The range of permissible values is -PI/2 to PI/2

fields.name: residualPower
fields.comment: This field shall specify the residual effective radiated power in the blanking sector in dBm.

classes.name: AngleDeception
classes.comment: The Angle Deception attribute record may be used to communicate discrete values that are associated with angle deception jamming that cannot be referenced to an emitter mode. The values provided in the record records (provided in the associated Electromagnetic Emission PDU). (The victim radar beams are those that are targeted by the jammer.) Section 6.2.21.2.2

fields.name: recordLength
fields.comment: the length of the record in octets.

fields.name: emitterNumber
fields.comment: indicates the emitter system for which the angle deception values are applicable.

fields.name: beamNumber
fields.comment: indicates the jamming beam for which the angle deception values are applicable.

fields.name: stateIndicator
fields.comment: This field shall be used to indicate if angle deception data have changed since issuance of the last Angle Deception attribute record for this beam, if the Angle Deception attribute record is part of a heartbeat update to meet periodic update requirements or if the angle deception data for the beam has ceased.

fields.name: azimuthOffset
fields.comment: This field indicates the relative azimuth angle at which the deceptive radar returns are centered. This angle is measured in the X-Y plane of the victim radar's entity coordinate system (see 1.4.3). This angle is measured in radians from the victim radar entity's azimuth for the true jam- mer position to the center of the range of azimuths in which deceptive radar returns are perceived as shown in Figure 43. Positive and negative values indicate that the perceived positions of the jammer are right and left of the true position of the jammer, respectively. The range of permissible values is -PI to PI

fields.name: azimuthWidth
fields.comment: indicates the range of azimuths (in radians) through which the deceptive radar returns are perceived, centered on the azimuth offset as shown in Figure 43. The range of permissible values is 0 to 2PI radians

fields.name: azimuthPullRate
fields.comment: This field indicates the rate (in radians per second) at which the Azimuth Offset value is changing. Positive and negative values indicate that the Azimuth Offset is moving to the right or left, respectively.

fields.name: azimuthPullAcceleration
fields.comment: This field indicates the rate (in radians per second squared) at which the Azimuth Pull Rate value is changing. Azimuth Pull Acceleration is defined as positive to the right and negative to the left.

fields.name: elevationOffset
fields.comment: This field indicates the relative elevation angle at which the deceptive radar returns begin. This angle is measured as an angle with respect to the X-Y plane of the victim radar's entity coordinate system (see 1.4.3). This angle is measured in radians from the victim radar entity's eleva- tion for the true jammer position to the center of the range of elevations in which deceptive radar returns are perceived as shown in Figure 44. Positive and negative values indicate that the perceived positions of the jammer are above and below the true position of the jammer, respectively. The range of permissible values is -PI/2 to PI/2

fields.name: elevationWidth
fields.comment: This field indicates the range of elevations (in radians) through which the decep- tive radar returns are perceived, centered on the elevation offset as shown in Figure 44. The range of permissible values is 0 to PI radians

fields.name: elevationPullRate
fields.comment: This field indicates the rate (in radians per second) at which the Elevation Off- set value is changing. Positive and negative values indicate that the Elevation Offset is moving up or down, respectively.

fields.name: elevationPullAcceleration
fields.comment: This field indicates the rate (in radians per second squared) at which the Elevation Pull Rate value is changing. Elevation Pull Acceleration is defined as positive to upward and negative downward.

classes.name: FalseTargetsAttribute
classes.comment: The False Targets attribute record shall be used to communicate discrete values that are associated with false targets jamming that cannot be referenced to an emitter mode. The values provided in the False Targets attri- bute record shall be considered valid only for the victim radar beams listed in the jamming beam's Track/Jam Data records (provided in the associated Electromagnetic Emission PDU). Section 6.2.21.3

fields.name: recordLength
fields.comment: the length of the record in octets.

fields.name: emitterNumber
fields.comment: This field indicates the emitter system generating the false targets.

fields.name: beamNumber
fields.comment: This field indicates the jamming beam generating the false targets.

fields.name: stateIndicator
fields.comment: This field shall be used to indicate if false target data have changed since issuance of the last False Targets attribute record for this beam, if the False Targets attribute record is part of a heartbeat update to meet periodic update requirements or if false target data for the beam has ceased.

fields.name: falseTargetCount
fields.comment: This field indicates the jamming beam generating the false targets.

fields.name: walkSpeed
fields.comment: This field shall specify the speed (in meters per second) at which false targets move toward the victim radar. Negative values shall indicate a velocity away from the victim radar.

fields.name: walkAcceleration
fields.comment: This field shall specify the rate (in meters per second squared) at which false tar- gets accelerate toward the victim radar. Negative values shall indicate an acceleration direction away from the victim radar.

fields.name: maximumWalkDistance
fields.comment: This field shall specify the distance (in meters) that a false target is to walk before it pauses in range.

fields.name: keepTime
fields.comment: This field shall specify the time (in seconds) that a false target is to be held at the Maxi- mum Walk Distance before it resets to its initial position.

fields.name: echoSpacing
fields.comment: TThis field shall specify the distance between false targets in meters. Positive values for this field shall indicate that second and subsequent false targets are initially placed at increasing ranges from the victim radar.

classes.name: EEFundamentalParameterData
classes.comment: Contains electromagnetic emmission regeneration parameters that are variable throught a scenario. Section 6.2.22.

fields.name: frequency
fields.comment: center frequency of the emission in hertz.

fields.name: frequencyRange
fields.comment: Bandwidth of the frequencies corresponding to the fequency field.

fields.name: effectiveRadiatedPower
fields.comment: Effective radiated power for the emission in DdBm. For a radar noise jammer, indicates the peak of the transmitted power.

fields.name: pulseRepetitionFrequency
fields.comment: Average repetition frequency of the emission in hertz.

fields.name: pulseWidth
fields.comment: Average pulse width  of the emission in microseconds.

classes.name: EmitterSystem
classes.comment: This field shall specify information about a particular emitter system. Section 6.2.23.

fields.name: emitterName
fields.comment: Name of the emitter, 16 bit enumeration

fields.name: emitterFunction
fields.comment: function of the emitter, 8 bit enumeration

classes.name: EngineFuel
classes.comment: Information about an entity's engine fuel. Section 6.2.24.

fields.name: fuelQuantity
fields.comment: Fuel quantity, units specified by next field

fields.name: fuelMeasurementUnits
fields.comment: Units in which the fuel is measured

fields.name: fuelLocation
fields.comment: Location of fuel as related to entity. See section 14 of EBV document

classes.name: EngineFuelReload
classes.comment: For each type or location of engine fuell, this record specifies the type, location, fuel measurement units, and reload quantity and maximum quantity. Section 6.2.25.

fields.name: standardQuantityReloadTime
fields.comment: seconds normally required to to reload standard qty

fields.name: maximumQuantityReloadTime
fields.comment: seconds normally required to to reload maximum qty

fields.name: fuelLocation
fields.comment: fuel  location as related to the entity

classes.name: EntityIdentifier
classes.comment: Entity Identifier. Unique ID for entities in the world. Consists of an simulation address and a entity number. Section 6.2.28.

classes.name: EntityID
classes.comment: more laconically named EntityIdentifier

classes.name: EntityMarking
classes.comment: Specifies the character set used inthe first byte, followed by 11 characters of text data. Section 6.29

classes.name: EntityType
classes.comment: Identifies the type of Entity

fields.name: domain
fields.comment: Domain of entity (air, surface, subsurface, space, etc)

fields.name: country
fields.comment: country to which the design of the entity is attributed

fields.name: specific
fields.comment: specific info based on subcategory field. Renamed from specific because that is a reserved word in SQL.

classes.name: EnvironmentGeneral
classes.comment: Information about a geometry, a state associated with a geometry, a bounding volume, or an associated entity ID. NOTE: this class requires hand coding. 6.2.31

fields.name: index
fields.comment: Identify the sequentially numbered record index

fields.name: padding2
fields.comment: padding to bring the total size up to a 64 bit boundry

classes.name: Environment
classes.comment: Incomplete environment record; requires hand coding to fix. Section 6.2.31.1

fields.name: length
fields.comment: length, in bits, of the record

fields.name: index
fields.comment: identifies the sequntially numbered record index

classes.name: EnvironmentType
classes.comment: Description of environmental data in environmental process and gridded data PDUs. Section 6.2.32

fields.name: domain
fields.comment: Domain of entity (air, surface, subsurface, space, etc)

fields.name: specific
fields.comment: specific info based on subcategory field

classes.name: EulerAngles
classes.comment: Three floating point values representing an orientation, psi, theta, and phi, aka the euler angles, in radians. Section 6.2.33

classes.name: EventIdentifier
classes.comment: Identifies an event in the world. Use this format for every PDU EXCEPT the LiveEntityPdu. Section 6.2.34.

classes.name: EventIdentifierLiveEntity
classes.comment: Identifies an event in the world. Use this format for ONLY the LiveEntityPdu. Section 6.2.34.

classes.name: Expendable
classes.comment: An entity's expendable (chaff, flares, etc) information. Section 6.2.36

classes.name: ExpendableReload
classes.comment: An entity's expendable (chaff, flares, etc) information. Section 6.2.37

classes.name: FixedDatum
classes.comment: Fixed Datum Record. Section 6.2.38

fields.name: fixedDatumID
fields.comment: ID of the fixed datum, an enumeration

classes.name: FundamentalOperationalData
classes.comment: Basic operational data for IFF. Section 6.2.40.

classes.name: GridAxis
classes.comment: Grid axis record for fixed data. Section 6.2.41

fields.name: domainInitialXi
fields.comment: coordinate of the grid origin or initial value

fields.name: domainFinalXi
fields.comment: coordinate of the endpoint or final value

fields.name: domainPointsXi
fields.comment: The number of grid points along the Xi domain axis for the enviornmental state data

fields.name: interleafFactor
fields.comment: interleaf factor along the domain axis.

fields.name: numberOfPointsOnXiAxis
fields.comment: Number of grid locations along Xi axis

fields.name: initialIndex
fields.comment: initial grid point for the current pdu

classes.name: GridAxisDescriptorVariable
classes.comment: Grid axis descriptor fo variable spacing axis data. NOT COMPLETE. Need padding to 64 bit boundary.

fields.name: domainInitialXi
fields.comment: coordinate of the grid origin or initial value

fields.name: domainFinalXi
fields.comment: coordinate of the endpoint or final value

fields.name: domainPointsXi
fields.comment: The number of grid points along the Xi domain axis for the enviornmental state data

fields.name: interleafFactor
fields.comment: interleaf factor along the domain axis.

fields.name: numberOfPointsOnXiAxis
fields.comment: Number of grid locations along Xi axis

fields.name: initialIndex
fields.comment: initial grid point for the current pdu

fields.name: coordinateScaleXi
fields.comment: value that linearly scales the coordinates of the grid locations for the xi axis

fields.name: coordinateOffsetXi
fields.comment: The constant offset value that shall be applied to the grid locations for the xi axis

classes.name: GroupID
classes.comment: Unique designation of a group of entities contained in the isGroupOfPdu. Represents a group of entities rather than a single entity. Section 6.2.43

classes.name: IFFData
classes.comment: repeating element if IFF Data specification record

fields.name: recordLength
fields.comment: length of record. Should be padded to 32 bit boundary.

classes.name: IffDataSpecification
classes.comment: Requires hand coding to be useful. Section 6.2.43

classes.name: IFFFundamentalParameterData
classes.comment: Fundamental IFF atc data. Section 6.2.45

classes.name: IntercomCommunicationsParameters
classes.comment: Intercom communcations parameters. Section 6.2.47.  This requires hand coding

classes.name: IntercomIdentifier
classes.comment: Unique designation of an attached or unattached intercom in an event or exercirse. Section 6.2.48

classes.name: IOCommunicationsNode
classes.comment: A communications node that is part of a simulted communcations network. Section 6.2.49.2

classes.name: IOEffect
classes.comment: Effect of IO on an entity. Section 6.2.49.3

classes.name: CommunicationsNodeID
classes.comment: Identity of a communications node. Section 6.2.48.4

classes.name: JammingTechnique
classes.comment: Jamming technique. Section 6.2.49

classes.name: LaunchedMunitionRecord
classes.comment: Identity of a communications node. Section 6.2.50

classes.name: LayerHeader
classes.comment: The identification of the additional information layer number, layer-specific information, and the length of the layer. Section 6.2.51

fields.name: layerSpecificInformation
fields.comment: field shall specify layer-specific information that varies by System Type (see 6.2.86) and Layer Number.

fields.name: length
fields.comment: This field shall specify the length in octets of the layer, including the Layer Header record

classes.name: LinearSegmentParameter
classes.comment: The specification of an individual segment of a linear segment synthetic environment object in a Linear Object State PDU Section 6.2.52

fields.name: segmentNumber
fields.comment: the individual segment of the linear segment

fields.name: segmentModification
fields.comment: whether a modification has been made to the point object's location or orientation

fields.name: generalSegmentAppearance
fields.comment: general dynamic appearance attributes of the segment. This record shall be defined as a 16-bit record of enumerations. The values defined for this record are included in Section 12 of SISO-REF-010.

fields.name: specificSegmentAppearance
fields.comment: This field shall specify specific dynamic appearance attributes of the segment. This record shall be defined as a 32-bit record of enumerations.

fields.name: segmentLocation
fields.comment: This field shall specify the location of the linear segment in the simulated world and shall be represented by a World Coordinates record

fields.name: segmentOrientation
fields.comment: orientation of the linear segment about the segment location and shall be represented by a Euler Angles record

fields.name: segmentLength
fields.comment: length of the linear segment, in meters, extending in the positive X direction

fields.name: segmentWidth
fields.comment: The total width of the linear segment, in meters, shall be specified by a 16-bit unsigned integer. One-half of the width shall extend in the positive Y direction, and one-half of the width shall extend in the negative Y direction.

fields.name: segmentHeight
fields.comment: The height of the linear segment, in meters, above ground shall be specified by a 16-bit unsigned integer.

fields.name: segmentDepth
fields.comment: The depth of the linear segment, in meters, below ground level

classes.name: LiveEntityIdentifier
classes.comment: The unique designation of each entity in an event or exercise that is contained in a Live Entity PDU. Section 6.2.54

classes.name: LiveSimulationAddress
classes.comment: A simulation's designation associated with all Live Entity IDs contained in Live Entity PDUs. Section 6.2.55

fields.name: liveSiteNumber
fields.comment: facility, installation, organizational unit or geographic location may have multiple sites associated with it. The Site Number is the first component of the Live Simulation Address, which defines a live simulation.

fields.name: liveApplicationNumber
fields.comment: An application associated with a live site is termed a live application. Each live application participating in an event

classes.name: MineEntityIdentifier
classes.comment: The unique designation of a mine contained in the Minefield Data PDU. No espdus are issued for mine entities.  Section 6.2.55

classes.name: MinefieldIdentifier
classes.comment: The unique designation of a minefield Section 6.2.56

classes.name: MinefieldSensorType
classes.comment: Information about a minefield sensor. Section 6.2.57

fields.name: sensorType
fields.comment: sensor type. bit fields 0-3 are the type category, 4-15 are teh subcategory

classes.name: ModulationParameters
classes.comment: Modulation parameters associated with a specific radio system. INCOMPLETE. 6.2.58

classes.name: ModulationType
classes.comment: Information about the type of modulation used for radio transmission. 6.2.59

fields.name: spreadSpectrum
fields.comment: This field shall indicate the spread spectrum technique or combination of spread spectrum techniques in use. Bit field. 0=freq hopping, 1=psuedo noise, time hopping=2, reamining bits unused

fields.name: majorModulation
fields.comment: the major classification of the modulation type.

fields.name: detail
fields.comment: provide certain detailed information depending upon the major modulation type

fields.name: radioSystem
fields.comment: the radio system associated with this Transmitter PDU and shall be used as the basis to interpret other fields whose values depend on a specific radio system.

classes.name: Munition
classes.comment: An entity's munition (e.g., bomb, missile) information shall be represented by one or more Munition records. For each type or location of munition, this record shall specify the type, location, quantity and status of munitions that an entity contains. Section 6.2.60

fields.name: munitionType
fields.comment: This field shall identify the entity type of the munition. See section 6.2.30.

fields.name: station
fields.comment: the station or launcher to which the munition is assigned. See Annex I

fields.name: quantity
fields.comment: the quantity remaining of this munition.

fields.name: munitionStatus
fields.comment: the status of the munition. It shall be represented by an 8-bit enumeration.

classes.name: MunitionReload
classes.comment: indicate weapons (munitions) previously communicated via the Munition record. Section 6.2.61

fields.name: munitionType
fields.comment: This field shall identify the entity type of the munition. See section 6.2.30.

fields.name: station
fields.comment: the station or launcher to which the munition is assigned. See Annex I

fields.name: standardQuantity
fields.comment: the standard quantity of this munition type normally loaded at this station/launcher if a station/launcher is specified.

fields.name: maximumQuantity
fields.comment: the maximum quantity of this munition type that this station/launcher is capable of holding when a station/launcher is specified

fields.name: standardQuantityReloadTime
fields.comment: numer of seconds of sim time required to reload the std qty

fields.name: maximumQuantityReloadTime
fields.comment: the number of seconds of sim time required to reload the max possible quantity

classes.name: NamedLocationIdentification
classes.comment: Information about the discrete positional relationship of the part entity with respect to the its host entity Section 6.2.62

fields.name: stationName
fields.comment: the station name within the host at which the part entity is located. If the part entity is On Station, this field shall specify the representation of the part's location data fields. This field shall be specified by a 16-bit enumeration

fields.name: stationNumber
fields.comment: the number of the particular wing station, cargo hold etc., at which the part is attached.

classes.name: ObjectIdentifier
classes.comment: The unique designation of an environmental object. Section 6.2.63

classes.name: ObjectType
classes.comment: The unique designation of an environmental object. Section 6.2.64

fields.name: domain
fields.comment: Domain of entity (air, surface, subsurface, space, etc)

fields.name: objectKind
fields.comment: country to which the design of the entity is attributed

classes.name: OwnershipStatus
classes.comment: used to convey entity and conflict status information associated with transferring ownership of an entity. Section 6.2.65

fields.name: ownershipStatus
fields.comment: The ownership and/or ownership conflict status of the entity represented by the Entity ID field.

classes.name: PduHeader
classes.comment: Not used. The PDU Header Record is directly incoroporated into the PDU class. Here for completness only. Section 6.2.66

fields.name: pduType
fields.comment: Type of pdu, unique for each PDU class

fields.name: protocolFamily
fields.comment: value that refers to the protocol family, eg SimulationManagement, etc

fields.name: pduLength
fields.comment: Length, in bytes, of the PDU. Changed name from length to avoid use of Hibernate QL reserved word.

fields.name: pduStatus
fields.comment: PDU Status Record. Described in 6.2.67. This field is not present in earlier DIS versions

classes.name: PduStatus
classes.comment: PDU Status. These are a series of bit fields. Represented here as just a byte. Section 6.2.67

fields.name: pduStatus
fields.comment: Bit fields. The semantics of the bit fields depend on the PDU type

classes.name: PropulsionSystemData
classes.comment: contains information describing the propulsion systems of the entity. This information shall be provided for each active propulsion system defined. Section 6.2.68

classes.name: ProtocolMode
classes.comment: Bit field used to identify minefield data. bits 14-15 are a 2-bit enum, other bits unused. Section 6.2.69

classes.name: RadioIdentifier
classes.comment: The unique designation of an attached or unattached radio in an event or exercise Section 6.2.70

classes.name: RadioType
classes.comment: Identifies the type of radio. Section 6.2.71

fields.name: domain
fields.comment: Domain of entity (air, surface, subsurface, space, etc)

fields.name: country
fields.comment: country to which the design of the entity is attributed

fields.name: subcategory
fields.comment: specific info based on subcategory field

classes.name: RecordQuerySpecification
classes.comment: The identification of the records being queried 6.2.72

fields.name: records
fields.comment: variable length list of 32 bit records

classes.name: RecordSpecificationElement
classes.comment: Synthetic record, made up from section 6.2.72. This is used to acheive a repeating variable list element.

fields.name: recordID
fields.comment: the data structure used to convey the parameter values of the record for each record. 32 bit enumeration.

fields.name: recordSetSerialNumber
fields.comment: the serial number of the first record in the block of records

fields.name: recordLength
fields.comment: the length, in bits, of the record. Note, bits, not bytes.

fields.name: recordCount
fields.comment: the number of records included in the record set

fields.name: recordValues
fields.comment: the concatenated records of the format specified by the Record ID field. The length of this field is the Record Length multiplied by the Record Count, in units of bits. ^^^This is wrong--variable sized data records, bit values. THis MUST be patched after generation.

fields.name: pad4
fields.comment: Padding of 0 to 31 unused bits as required for 32-bit alignment of the Record Set field. ^^^This is wrong--variable sized padding. MUST be patched post-code generation

classes.name: RecordSpecification
classes.comment: This record shall specify the number of record sets contained in the Record Specification record and the record details. Section 6.2.73.

classes.name: Relationship
classes.comment: The relationship of the part entity to its host entity. Section 6.2.74.

fields.name: nature
fields.comment: the nature or purpose for joining of the part entity to the host entity and shall be represented by a 16-bit enumeration

fields.name: position
fields.comment: the position of the part entity with respect to the host entity and shall be represented by a 16-bit enumeration

classes.name: RequestID
classes.comment: A monotonically increasing number inserted into all simulation managment PDUs. This should be a hand-coded thingie, maybe a singleton. Section 6.2.75

classes.name: SecondaryOperationalData
classes.comment: Additional operational data for an IFF emitting system and the number of IFF Fundamental Parameter Data records Section 6.2.76.

fields.name: operationalData1
fields.comment: additional operational characteristics of the IFF emitting system. Each 8-bit field will vary depending on the system type.

fields.name: operationalData2
fields.comment: additional operational characteristics of the IFF emitting system. Each 8-bit field will vary depending on the system type.

fields.name: numberOfIFFFundamentalParameterRecords
fields.comment: the number of IFF Fundamental Parameter Data records that follow

classes.name: Sensor
classes.comment: An entity's sensor information.  Section 6.2.77.

fields.name: sensorTypeSource
fields.comment: the source of the Sensor Type field

fields.name: sensorOnOffStatus
fields.comment: the on/off status of the sensor

fields.name: sensorType
fields.comment: the sensor type and shall be represented by a 16-bit enumeration.

fields.name: station
fields.comment: the station to which the sensor is assigned. A zero value shall indi- cate that this Sensor record is not associated with any particular station and represents the total quan- tity of this sensor for this entity. If this field is non-zero, it shall either reference an attached part or an articulated part

classes.name: SilentEntitySystem
classes.comment: information abou an enitity not producing espdus. Section 6.2.79

fields.name: numberOfEntities
fields.comment: number of the type specified by the entity type field

fields.name: numberOfAppearanceRecords
fields.comment: number of entity appearance records that follow

fields.name: appearanceRecordList
fields.comment: Variable length list of appearance records

classes.name: SimulationAddress
classes.comment: A Simulation Address record shall consist of the Site Identification number and the Application Identification number. Section 6.2.79

fields.name: site
fields.comment: A site is defined as a facility, installation, organizational unit or a geographic location that has one or more simulation applications capable of participating in a distributed event.

fields.name: application
fields.comment: An application is defined as a software program that is used to generate and process distributed simulation data including live, virtual and constructive data.

classes.name: SimulationIdentifier
classes.comment: The unique designation of a simulation when using the 48-bit identifier format shall be specified by the Sim- ulation Identifier record. The reason that the 48-bit format is required in addition to the 32-bit simulation address format that actually identifies a specific simulation is because some 48-bit identifier fields in PDUs may contain either an Object Identifier, such as an Entity ID, or a Simulation Identifier. Section 6.2.80

fields.name: referenceNumber
fields.comment: This field shall be set to zero as there is no reference number associated with a Simulation Identifier.

classes.name: SimulationManagementPduHeader
classes.comment: First part of a simulation management (SIMAN) PDU and SIMAN-Reliability (SIMAN-R) PDU. Sectionn 6.2.81

fields.name: originatingID
fields.comment: IDs the simulation or entity, etiehr a simulation or an entity. Either 6.2.80 or 6.2.28

fields.name: receivingID
fields.comment: simulation, all simulations, a special ID, or an entity. See 5.6.5 and 5.12.4

classes.name: StandardVariableSpecification
classes.comment: Does not work, and causes failure in anything it is embedded in. Section 6.2.83

fields.name: standardVariables
fields.comment: variable length list of standard variables, The class type and length here are WRONG and will cause the incorrect serialization of any class in whihc it is embedded.

classes.name: StorageFuel
classes.comment: Information about an entity's engine fuel. Section 6.2.84.

fields.name: fuelQuantity
fields.comment: Fuel quantity, units specified by next field

fields.name: fuelMeasurementUnits
fields.comment: Units in which the fuel is measured

fields.name: fuelLocation
fields.comment: Location of fuel as related to entity. See section 14 of EBV document

classes.name: StorageFuelReload
classes.comment: For each type or location of Storage Fuel, this record shall specify the type, location, fuel measure- ment units, reload quantity and maximum quantity for storage fuel either for the whole entity or a specific storage fuel location (tank). Section 6.2.85.

fields.name: standardQuantity
fields.comment: the standard quantity of this fuel type normally loaded at this station/launcher if a station/launcher is specified. If the Station/Launcher field is set to zero, then this is the total quantity of this fuel type that would be present in a standard reload of all appli- cable stations/launchers associated with this entity.

fields.name: maximumQuantity
fields.comment: the maximum quantity of this fuel type that this sta- tion/launcher is capable of holding when a station/launcher is specified. This would be the value used when a maximum reload was desired to be set for this station/launcher. If the Station/launcher field is set to zero, then this is the maximum quantity of this fuel type that would be present on this entity at all stations/launchers that can accept this fuel type.

fields.name: standardQuantityReloadTime
fields.comment: the seconds normally required to reload the standard quantity of this fuel type at this specific station/launcher. When the Station/Launcher field is set to zero, this shall be the time it takes to perform a standard quantity reload of this fuel type at all applicable stations/launchers for this entity.

fields.name: maximumQuantityReloadTime
fields.comment: the seconds normally required to reload the maximum possible quantity of this fuel type at this station/launcher. When the Station/Launcher field is set to zero, this shall be the time it takes to perform a maximum quantity load/reload of this fuel type at all applicable stations/launchers for this entity.

fields.name: fuelLocation
fields.comment: Location of fuel as related to entity. See section 14 of EBV document

classes.name: SupplyQuantity
classes.comment: A supply, and the amount of that supply. Section 6.2.86

fields.name: quantity
fields.comment: the number of units of a supply type.

classes.name: SystemIdentifier
classes.comment: The ID of the IFF emitting system. NOT COMPLETE. Section 6.2.87

fields.name: systemType
fields.comment: general type of emitting system, an enumeration

fields.name: systemName
fields.comment: named type of system, an enumeration

fields.name: systemMode
fields.comment: mode of operation for the system, an enumeration

fields.name: changeOptions
fields.comment: status of this PDU, see section 6.2.15

classes.name: Timestamp
classes.comment: LSB is absolute or relative timestamp. Scale is 2^31 - 1 divided into one hour.

classes.name: TotalRecordSets
classes.comment: Total number of record sets contained in a logical set of one or more PDUs. Used to transfer ownership, etc Section 6.2.88

classes.name: TrackJamData
classes.comment: Track-Jam data Section 6.2.89

fields.name: entityID
fields.comment: the entity tracked or illumated, or an emitter beam targeted with jamming

fields.name: emitterNumber
fields.comment: Emitter system associated with the entity

classes.name: UAFundamentalParameter
classes.comment: Regeneration parameters for active emission systems that are variable throughout a scenario. Section 6.2.91

fields.name: activeEmissionParameterIndex
fields.comment: Which database record shall be used. An enumeration from EBV document

fields.name: scanPattern
fields.comment: The type of scan pattern, If not used, zero. An enumeration from EBV document

fields.name: beamCenterAzimuthHorizontal
fields.comment: center azimuth bearing of th emain beam. In radians.

fields.name: azimuthalBeamwidthHorizontal
fields.comment: Horizontal beamwidth of th emain beam Meastued at the 3dB down point of peak radiated power. In radians.

fields.name: beamCenterDepressionElevation
fields.comment: center of the d/e angle of th emain beam relative to the stablised de angle of the target. In radians.

fields.name: beamwidthDownElevation
fields.comment: vertical beamwidth of the main beam. Meastured at the 3dB down point of peak radiated power. In radians.

classes.name: UnattachedIdentifier
classes.comment: The unique designation of one or more unattached radios in an event or exercise Section 6.2.91

classes.name: VariableDatum
classes.comment: the variable datum type, the datum length, and the value for that variable datum type. NOT COMPLETE. Section 6.2.93

fields.name: variableDatumID
fields.comment: Type of variable datum to be transmitted. 32 bit enumeration defined in EBV

fields.name: variableDatumLength
fields.comment: Length, IN BITS, of the variable datum.

classes.name: VariableParameter
classes.comment: specification of additional information associated with an entity or detonation, not otherwise accounted for in a PDU 6.2.94.1

fields.name: recordType
fields.comment: the identification of the Variable Parameter record. Enumeration from EBV

fields.name: variableParameterFields1
fields.comment: Variable parameter data fields. Two doubles minus one byte

classes.name: ArticulatedParts
classes.comment: articulated parts for movable parts and a combination of moveable/attached parts of an entity. Section 6.2.94.2

fields.name: recordType
fields.comment: the identification of the Variable Parameter record. Enumeration from EBV

fields.name: changeIndicator
fields.comment: indicate the change of any parameter for any articulated part. Starts at zero, incremented for each change

fields.name: partAttachedTo
fields.comment: the identification of the articulated part to which this articulation parameter is attached. This field shall be specified by a 16-bit unsigned integer. This field shall contain the value zero if the articulated part is attached directly to the entity.

fields.name: parameterType
fields.comment: the type of parameter represented, 32 bit enumeration

fields.name: parameterValue
fields.comment: The definition of the 64 bits shall be determined based on the type of parameter specified in the Parameter Type field

classes.name: AttachedParts
classes.comment: Removable parts that may be attached to an entity.  Section 6.2.93.3

fields.name: recordType
fields.comment: the identification of the Variable Parameter record. Enumeration from EBV

fields.name: detachedIndicator
fields.comment: 0 = attached, 1 = detached. See I.2.3.1 for state transition diagram

fields.name: partAttachedTo
fields.comment: the identification of the articulated part to which this articulation parameter is attached. This field shall be specified by a 16-bit unsigned integer. This field shall contain the value zero if the articulated part is attached directly to the entity.

fields.name: parameterType
fields.comment: The location or station to which the part is attached

fields.name: parameterValue
fields.comment: The definition of the 64 bits shall be determined based on the type of parameter specified in the Parameter Type field

classes.name: EntityAssociation
classes.comment: Association or disassociation of two entities.  Section 6.2.94.4.3

fields.name: recordType
fields.comment: the identification of the Variable Parameter record. Enumeration from EBV

fields.name: changeIndicator
fields.comment: Indicates if this VP has changed since last issuance

fields.name: associationStatus
fields.comment: Indicates association status between two entities; 8 bit enum

fields.name: entityID
fields.comment: Object ID of entity associated with this entity

fields.name: ownStationLocation
fields.comment: Station location on one's own entity. EBV doc.

fields.name: physicalConnectionType
fields.comment: Type of physical connection. EBV doc

fields.name: groupMemberType
fields.comment: Type of member the entity is within th egroup

fields.name: groupNumber
fields.comment: Group if any to which the entity belongs

classes.name: EntityTypeVP
classes.comment: Association or disassociation of two entities.  Section 6.2.94.5

fields.name: recordType
fields.comment: the identification of the Variable Parameter record. Enumeration from EBV

fields.name: changeIndicator
fields.comment: Indicates if this VP has changed since last issuance

classes.name: SeparationVP
classes.comment: Physical separation of an entity from another entity.  Section 6.2.94.6

fields.name: recordType
fields.comment: the identification of the Variable Parameter record. Enumeration from EBV

fields.name: preEntityIndicator
fields.comment: Whether the entity existed prior to separation EBV

classes.name: VariableTransmitterParameters
classes.comment: Relates to radios. NOT COMPLETE. Section 6.2.94

fields.name: recordType
fields.comment: Type of VTP. Enumeration from EBV

classes.name: Vector3Float
classes.comment: Three floating point values, x, y, and z. Section 6.2.95

classes.name: Vector2Float
classes.comment: Two floating point values, x, y

classes.name: VectoringNozzleSystem
classes.comment: Operational data for describing the vectoring nozzle systems Section 6.2.96

classes.name: Vector3Double
classes.comment: Three double precision floating point values, x, y, and z. Used for world coordinates Section 6.2.97.

classes.name: DeadReckoningParameters
classes.comment: Not specified in the standard. This is used by the ESPDU

fields.name: deadReckoningAlgorithm
fields.comment: Algorithm to use in computing dead reckoning. See EBV doc.

fields.name: parameters
fields.comment: Dead reckoning parameters. Contents depends on algorithm.

classes.name: EightByteChunk
classes.comment: 64 bit piece of data

classes.name: OneByteChunk
classes.comment: 8 bit piece of data

classes.name: TwoByteChunk
classes.comment: 16 bit piece of data

classes.name: FourByteChunk
classes.comment: 32 bit piece of data

classes.name: PduStream
classes.comment: Non-DIS class, used on SQL databases. This is not in the DIS standard but can be helpful when saving DIS to a SQL database, particularly in Java.

fields.name: description
fields.comment: Longish description of this PDU stream

fields.name: name
fields.comment: short description of this PDU stream

fields.name: startTime
fields.comment: Start time of recording, in Unix time (seconds since epoch)

fields.name: stopTime
fields.comment: stop time of recording, in Unix time (seconds since epoch)

classes.name: PduSuperclass
classes.comment: The superclass for all PDUs, including classic and Live Entity (LE) PDUs. This incorporates the PduHeader record, section 7.2.2

fields.name: pduType
fields.comment: Type of pdu, unique for each PDU class

fields.name: protocolFamily
fields.comment: value that refers to the protocol family, eg SimulationManagement, et

fields.name: length
fields.comment: Length, in bytes, of the PDU

classes.name: Pdu
classes.comment: Adds some fields to the the classic PDU

fields.name: pduStatus
fields.comment: PDU Status Record. Described in 6.2.67. This field is not present in earlier DIS versions

classes.name: LiveEntityPdu
classes.comment: The live entity PDUs have a header with some different field names, but the same length. Section 9.3.2

fields.name: subprotocolNumber
fields.comment: Subprotocol used to decode the PDU. Section 13 of EBV.

classes.name: EntityInformationFamilyPdu
classes.comment: Section 5.3.3. Common superclass for EntityState, Collision, collision-elastic, and entity state update PDUs. This should be abstract. COMPLETE

classes.name: EntityStatePdu
classes.comment: Represents the postion and state of one entity in the world. Section 7.2.2. COMPLETE

fields.name: entityID
fields.comment: Unique ID for an entity that is tied to this state information

fields.name: forceId
fields.comment: What force this entity is affiliated with, eg red, blue, neutral, etc

fields.name: numberOfVariableParameters
fields.comment: How many variable parameters are in the variable length list. In earlier versions of DIS these were known as articulation parameters

fields.name: entityType
fields.comment: Describes the type of entity in the world

fields.name: entityLinearVelocity
fields.comment: Describes the speed of the entity in the world

fields.name: entityLocation
fields.comment: describes the location of the entity in the world

fields.name: entityOrientation
fields.comment: describes the orientation of the entity, in euler angles

fields.name: entityAppearance
fields.comment: a series of bit flags that are used to help draw the entity, such as smoking, on fire, etc.

fields.name: marking
fields.comment: characters that can be used for debugging, or to draw unique strings on the side of entities in the world

fields.name: variableParameters
fields.comment: variable length list of variable parameters. In earlier DIS versions this was articulation parameters.

classes.name: FastEntityStatePdu
classes.comment: Represents the postion and state of one entity in the world. This is identical in function to entity state pdu, but generates less garbage to collect in the Java world. Section 7.2.2. COMPLETE

fields.name: forceId
fields.comment: what force this entity is affiliated with, eg red, blue, neutral, etc

fields.name: numberOfVariableParameters
fields.comment: How many variable (nee articulation) parameters are in the variable length list

fields.name: domain
fields.comment: Domain of entity (air, surface, subsurface, space, etc)

fields.name: country
fields.comment: country to which the design of the entity is attributed

fields.name: specific
fields.comment: specific info based on subcategory field

fields.name: altDomain
fields.comment: Domain of entity (air, surface, subsurface, space, etc)

fields.name: altCountry
fields.comment: country to which the design of the entity is attributed

fields.name: altSpecific
fields.comment: specific info based on subcategory field

fields.name: entityAppearance
fields.comment: a series of bit flags that are used to help draw the entity, such as smoking, on fire, etc.

fields.name: deadReckoningAlgorithm
fields.comment: enumeration of what dead reckoning algorighm to use

fields.name: otherParameters
fields.comment: other parameters to use in the dead reckoning algorithm

fields.name: marking
fields.comment: characters that can be used for debugging, or to draw unique strings on the side of entities in the world

fields.name: variableParameters
fields.comment: variable length list of variable parameters. In earlier versions of DIS these were known as articulation parameters

classes.name: CollisionPdu
classes.comment: Section 7.2.3 Collisions between entities shall be communicated by issuing a Collision PDU. COMPLETE

fields.name: issuingEntityID
fields.comment: This field shall identify the entity that is issuing the PDU, and shall be represented by an Entity Identifier record (see 6.2.28).

fields.name: collidingEntityID
fields.comment: This field shall identify the entity that has collided with the issuing entity (see 5.3.3.4). This field shall be represented by an Entity Identifier record (see 6.2.28).

fields.name: eventID
fields.comment: This field shall contain an identification generated by the issuing simulation application to associate related collision events. This field shall be represented by an Event Identifier record (see 6.2.34).

fields.name: collisionType
fields.comment: This field shall identify the type of collision. The Collision Type field shall be represented by an 8-bit record of enumerations

fields.name: velocity
fields.comment: This field shall contain the velocity (at the time the collision is detected) of the issuing entity. The velocity shall be represented in world coordinates. This field shall be represented by the Linear Velocity Vector record [see 6.2.95 item c)].

fields.name: mass
fields.comment: This field shall contain the mass of the issuing entity, and shall be represented by a 32-bit floating point number representing kilograms.

fields.name: location
fields.comment: This field shall specify the location of the collision with respect to the entity with which the issuing entity collided. The Location field shall be represented by an Entity Coordinate Vector record [see 6.2.95 item a)].

classes.name: CollisionElasticPdu
classes.comment: Information about elastic collisions in a DIS exercise shall be communicated using a Collision-Elastic PDU. Section 7.2.4. COMPLETE

fields.name: issuingEntityID
fields.comment: This field shall identify the entity that is issuing the PDU and shall be represented by an Entity Identifier record (see 6.2.28)

fields.name: collidingEntityID
fields.comment: This field shall identify the entity that has collided with the issuing entity. This field shall be a valid identifier of an entity or server capable of responding to the receipt of this Collision-Elastic PDU. This field shall be represented by an Entity Identifier record (see 6.2.28).

fields.name: collisionEventID
fields.comment: This field shall contain an identification generated by the issuing simulation application to associate related collision events. This field shall be represented by an Event Identifier record (see 6.2.34).

fields.name: contactVelocity
fields.comment: This field shall contain the velocity at the time the collision is detected at the point the collision is detected. The velocity shall be represented in world coordinates. This field shall be represented by the Linear Velocity Vector record [see 6.2.95 item c)]

fields.name: mass
fields.comment: This field shall contain the mass of the issuing entity and shall be represented by a 32-bit floating point number representing kilograms

fields.name: locationOfImpact
fields.comment: This field shall specify the location of the collision with respect to the entity with which the issuing entity collided. This field shall be represented by an Entity Coordinate Vector record [see 6.2.95 item a)].

fields.name: collisionIntermediateResultXX
fields.comment: These six records represent the six independent components of a positive semi-definite matrix formed by pre-multiplying and post-multiplying the tensor of inertia, by the anti-symmetric matrix generated by the moment arm, and shall be represented by 32-bit floating point numbers (see 5.3.4.4)

fields.name: unitSurfaceNormal
fields.comment: This record shall represent the normal vector to the surface at the point of collision detection. The surface normal shall be represented in world coordinates. This field shall be represented by an Entity Coordinate Vector record [see 6.2.95 item a)].

fields.name: coefficientOfRestitution
fields.comment: This field shall represent the degree to which energy is conserved in a collision and shall be represented by a 32-bit floating point number. In addition, it represents a free parameter by which simulation application developers may "tune" their collision interactions.

classes.name: EntityStateUpdatePdu
classes.comment: Nonstatic information about a particular entity may be communicated by issuing an Entity State Update PDU. Section 7.2.5. COMPLETE

fields.name: entityID
fields.comment: This field shall identify the entity issuing the PDU, and shall be represented by an Entity Identifier record (see 6.2.28).

fields.name: numberOfVariableParameters
fields.comment: This field shall specify the number of variable parameters present. This field shall be represented by an 8-bit unsigned integer (see Annex I).

fields.name: entityLinearVelocity
fields.comment: This field shall specify an entity's linear velocity. The coordinate system for an entity's linear velocity depends on the dead reckoning algorithm used. This field shall be represented by a Linear Velocity Vector record [see 6.2.95 item c)]).

fields.name: entityLocation
fields.comment: This field shall specify an entity's physical location in the simulated world and shall be represented by a World Coordinates record (see 6.2.97).

fields.name: entityOrientation
fields.comment: This field shall specify an entity's orientation and shall be represented by an Euler Angles record (see 6.2.33).

fields.name: entityAppearance
fields.comment: This field shall specify the dynamic changes to the entity's appearance attributes. This field shall be represented by an Entity Appearance record (see 6.2.26).

fields.name: variableParameters
fields.comment: This field shall specify the parameter values for each Variable Parameter record that is included (see 6.2.93 and Annex I).

classes.name: AttributePdu
classes.comment: Information about individual attributes for a particular entity, other object, or event may be communicated using an Attribute PDU. The Attribute PDU shall not be used to exchange data available in any other PDU except where explicitly mentioned in the PDU issuance instructions within this standard. See 5.3.6 for the information requirements and issuance and receipt rules for this PDU. Section 7.2.6. INCOMPLETE

fields.name: originatingSimulationAddress
fields.comment: This field shall identify the simulation issuing the Attribute PDU. It shall be represented by a Simulation Address record (see 6.2.79).

fields.name: attributeRecordPduType
fields.comment: This field shall represent the type of the PDU that is being extended or updated, if applicable. It shall be represented by an 8-bit enumeration.

fields.name: attributeRecordProtocolVersion
fields.comment: This field shall indicate the Protocol Version associated with the Attribute Record PDU Type. It shall be represented by an 8-bit enumeration.

fields.name: masterAttributeRecordType
fields.comment: This field shall contain the Attribute record type of the Attribute records in the PDU if they all have the same Attribute record type. It shall be represented by a 32-bit enumeration.

fields.name: actionCode
fields.comment: This field shall identify the action code applicable to this Attribute PDU. The Action Code shall apply to all Attribute records contained in the PDU. It shall be represented by an 8-bit enumeration.
